residue_numbering: 1-indexed positions in the 346-aa ProteinGym target sequence
source: UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot P11436 directly annotates Glu59, Lys134, and Cys166 as AmiE active-site residues. See data/proteingym/source_records/uniprot_P11436_AMIE_PSEAE_active_site.json.
evidence: UniProt annotates Glu59 as proton acceptor, Lys134 as proton donor, and Cys166 as nucleophile. Novo et al. 2002 report loss or strong reduction of activity for E59 and K134 mutants, and Farnaud et al. 1999 report loss of activity for C166 mutants. The ProteinGym AF2 structure also places Cys166 close to Glu59 and Lys134, with heavy-atom distances from Cys166 of approximately 3.44 A to Glu59 and 4.21 A to Lys134.
